content.problemStatement:
The country of Berland initially has NNN isolated cities, the iii-th city has a significance of AiA_iAi‚Äã. The President of Berland wants to connect all the cities. He can construct a bidirectional road of length LLL (L>0)(L > 0)(L>0) from city XXX to city YYY if (AX&AY&L)=L(A_X \& A_Y \& L) = L(AX‚Äã&AY‚Äã&L)=L, where &\&& represents the bitwise AND operator.

What is the minimum total length of roads he has to construct, in order to connect all the cities in Berland? Print ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it is impossible.

Note: City XXX and City YYY are said to be connected if there exists a sequence of cities C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CKC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_KC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CK‚Äã (K‚â•1)(K \ge 1)(K‚â•1) such that C1=XC_1 = XC1‚Äã=X, CK=YC_K = YCK‚Äã=Y, and there exists a road from CiC_iCi‚Äã to Ci+1C_{i+1}Ci+1‚Äã, (1‚â§i<K)(1 \le i < K)(1‚â§i<K). All the cities in Berland are said to be connected when every pair of cities in Berland are connected.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN ‚Äî the number of cities.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã ‚Äî the significance of each of the NNN cities.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum total length of roads to connect all the cities in Berland, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if it is impossible.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: The following roads with minimum total length can be constructed to connect Berland:

A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 111 and city 222, since (A1&A2&4)=4A_1 \& A_2 \& 4) = 4A1‚Äã&A2‚Äã&4)=4.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 222 and city 333, since (A2&A3&4)=4A_2 \& A_3 \& 4) = 4A2‚Äã&A3‚Äã&4)=4.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 333 and city 444, since (A3&A4&4)=4A_3 \& A_4 \& 4) = 4A3‚Äã&A4‚Äã&4)=4.

Test case 2: The following roads with minimum total length can be constructed to connect Berland:

A road with L=2L=2L=2 between city 111 and city 333, since (A1&A3&2)=2A_1 \& A_3 \& 2) = 2A1‚Äã&A3‚Äã&2)=2.
A road with L=4L=4L=4 between city 222 and city 333, since (A2&A3&4)=4A_2 \& A_3 \& 4) = 4A2‚Äã&A3‚Äã&4)=4.
